packet.name: E – Durham A, Florida A, Nebraska A

tossups.question: A cruel ruler of this city, who allegedly locked all his castle wells, would finally be murdered by Andrea Lampugnani. Niccolò Piccinino became a condottiero for a ruler of this city who lost the Battle of Anghiari (“ong-YAH-ree”) thanks to Niccolò’s command. Another ruler of this city was imprisoned in a cage after being betrayed by Swiss mercenaries at Novara. One family gained control over this city by ousting Napoleone della Torre. The assassination of Gian Galeazzo Maria (“jahn gah-lay-AHT-so Maria”) allowed a ruler of this city and ally of Charles VIII to consolidate power. A “Golden” Republic was established in this city after the death of the childless Filippo Maria Visconti. A ruler of this city was nicknamed “il Moro” and patronized da Vinci’s The Last Supper. For 10 points, the Sforzas ruled what capital of Lombardy?
tossups.answer: Milan [or Milano; accept Duchy of Milan] (Filippo Maria Visconti lost the Battle of Anghiari.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: Milan [or Milano; accept Duchy of Milan] (Filippo Maria Visconti lost the Battle of Anghiari.)
tossups.question_sanitized: A cruel ruler of this city, who allegedly locked all his castle wells, would finally be murdered by Andrea Lampugnani. Niccolo Piccinino became a condottiero for a ruler of this city who lost the Battle of Anghiari ("ong-YAH-ree") thanks to Niccolo's command. Another ruler of this city was imprisoned in a cage after being betrayed by Swiss mercenaries at Novara. One family gained control over this city by ousting Napoleone della Torre. The assassination of Gian Galeazzo Maria ("jahn gah-lay-AHT-so Maria") allowed a ruler of this city and ally of Charles VIII to consolidate power. A "Golden" Republic was established in this city after the death of the childless Filippo Maria Visconti. A ruler of this city was nicknamed "il Moro" and patronized da Vinci's The Last Supper. For 10 points, the Sforzas ruled what capital of Lombardy?

tossups.question: Maurizio Seracini claimed that he found a long-lost one of these objects based on the cryptic nearby phrase “Cerca trova” (“CHAIR-ka TRO-va”). One of these objects contained a “yellow veil” of dust before work by John Brealey that was criticized by the Spanish press. These objects can be supported by the techniques of lining and cradling. A 2019 paper by Laura Hendriks et al. used two microsamples from an object of this type to detect a (*) bomb peak of carbon-14 isotopes. After botched amateur work, one of these objects in Spain became nicknamed “Behold the Monkey.” Conservators discovered that dark varnish made an object of this type that was commissioned by a militia company appear to take place at nighttime. For 10 points, name these objects that are restored by institutions like the Prado.
tossups.answer: paintings [accept murals or frescoes; prompt on artworks] (The first clue refers to Da Vinci’s The Battle of Anghiari and the second to Velázquez’s Las Meninas.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: paintings [accept murals or frescoes; prompt on artworks] (The first clue refers to Da Vinci's The Battle of Anghiari and the second to Velazquez's Las Meninas.)

A 2019 paper by Laura Hendriks et al. used two microsamples from an object of this type to detect a (*) bomb peak of carbon-14 isotopes. After botched amateur work, one of these objects in Spain became nicknamed "Behold the Monkey." Conservators discovered that dark varnish made an object of this type that was commissioned by a militia company appear to take place at nighttime. For 10 points, name these objects that are restored by institutions like the Prado.

tossups.question: The phrase "Cerca Trova" appears on a green flag in a painting of one of these events by Giorgio Vasari for the Hall of the Five Hundred, possibly hinting that it sits in front of another depiction of one of these events. For his palace in Munich, Wilhelm IV of Bavaria commissioned eight paintings depicting these events. Peter Paul Rubens's Hippopotamus Hunt is likely modeled on a lost Leonardo da Vinci painting of one of these events. Niccolo da Tolentino wears a red hat and sits atop a horse in Paolo Uccello's first of three depictions of one of these events in San Romano. A floating tablet, the sun and the moon are all simultaneously visible in an Albrecht Altdorfer painting of, for 10 points, what type of event that Alexander the Great won at Issus?
tossups.answer: battles [accept The Battle of San Romano, The Battle of Alexander at Issus, or Battle of Anghiari; prompt on general answers like wars or conflicts; anti-prompt on parts of a battle, such as cavalry charges]
tossups.answer_sanitized: battles [accept The Battle of San Romano, The Battle of Alexander at Issus, or Battle of Anghiari; prompt on general answers like wars or conflicts; anti-prompt on parts of a battle, such as cavalry charges]
tossups.question_sanitized: The phrase "Cerca Trova" appears on a green flag in a painting of one of these events by Giorgio Vasari for the Hall of the Five Hundred, possibly hinting that it sits in front of another depiction of one of these events. A floating tablet, the sun and the moon are all simultaneously visible in an Albrecht Altdorfer painting of, for 10 points, what type of event that Alexander the Great won at Issus?

bonuses.leadin: Leonardo wrote several treatises in preparation for a colossal sculpture depicting this artistic subject; that sculpture was never completed after the seventy tons of bronze needed were used to build cannons instead. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this artistic subject, several of which were also depicted in a painting called “The Lost Leonardo.” That painting featuring several of these subjects was accidentally melted after charcoal stoves were used to try to dry it.

Leonardo’s unrealized horse sculpture was abandoned in favor of defending this city against a siege by Charles VIII. The horse was intended for this city’s ruler Ludovico Sforza, who also patronized Leonardo’s The Last Supper.

Leonardo’s Milan period produced the two versions of this painting. Both the Louvre and the British National Gallery version show the angel Uriel and John the Baptist as part of a triangular composition in the title setting.
answers: horses [or equestrian art; prompt on riders or soldiers by asking “what animal are they riding?”; prompt on battles or fights by asking “what creatures are involved in the fight?”] (The painting is Leonardo da Vinci’s The Battle of Anghiari.)
Virgin of the Rocks [or Madonna of the Rocks; accept anything with Virgin or Madonna and rocks]
answers_sanitized: horses [or equestrian art; prompt on riders or soldiers by asking "what animal are they riding?"; prompt on battles or fights by asking "what creatures are involved in the fight?"] (The painting is Leonardo da Vinci's The Battle of Anghiari.)
Virgin of the Rocks [or Madonna of the Rocks; accept anything with Virgin or Madonna and rocks]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Leonardo wrote several treatises in preparation for a colossal sculpture depicting this artistic subject; that sculpture was never completed after the seventy tons of bronze needed were used to build cannons instead. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this artistic subject, several of which were also depicted in a painting called "The Lost Leonardo." That painting featuring several of these subjects was accidentally melted after charcoal stoves were used to try to dry it.

Leonardo's unrealized horse sculpture was abandoned in favor of defending this city against a siege by Charles VIII. The horse was intended for this city's ruler Ludovico Sforza, who also patronized Leonardo's The Last Supper.

Leonardo's Milan period produced the two versions of this painting. Both the Louvre and the British National Gallery version show the angel Uriel and John the Baptist as part of a triangular composition in the title setting.

bonuses.leadin: The only knowledge of this painting’s layout comes from a drawing by Peter Paul Rubens, whose own Hippopotamus Hunt evidently bears similarities to this work. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this painting that its artist abandoned when his attempt to work with oil paints on the surface of a wall went disastrously awry.

The Battle of Anghiari is known as the “lost” painting of this Renaissance master, whose folly of painting oil-on-wall stemmed from his bad experience frescoing The Last Supper onto the refectory wall at the Santa Maria delle Grazie.

The Battle of Anghiari was painted in the Salone dei Cinquecento in this Florentine building. Michelangelo’s original David stood outside this building until 1873, when it was replaced by a facsimile.
answers: <u>Leonardo</u> da <u>Vinci</u> [or <u>Leonardo</u> di ser Piero da <u>Vinci</u>; accept any underlined portion]
<u>Palazzo Vecchio</u> [or <u>Palazzo della Signoria</u>; accept <u>Palazzo del Popolo</u> or <u>Palazzo dei Priori</u> or <u>Palazzo Ducale</u>; prompt on “<u>Old Palace</u>”]
answers_sanitized: Leonardo da Vinci [or Leonardo di ser Piero da Vinci; accept any underlined portion]
Palazzo Vecchio [or Palazzo della Signoria; accept Palazzo del Popolo or Palazzo dei Priori or Palazzo Ducale; prompt on "Old Palace"]

The Battle of Anghiari is known as the "lost" painting of this Renaissance master, whose folly of painting oil-on-wall stemmed from his bad experience frescoing The Last Supper onto the refectory wall at the Santa Maria delle Grazie.

The Battle of Anghiari was painted in the Salone dei Cinquecento in this Florentine building. Michelangelo's original David stood outside this building until 1873, when it was replaced by a facsimile.
